node.Title: GitHub Availability Report: December 2022
node.Description: In December, we did not experience any incidents that resulted in degraded performance across GitHub services. This report sheds light into an incident that impacted customers using GitHub Packages and GitHub Pages in November.

node.Title: What’s with all the ducks?
node.Description: What in the world do rubber ducks have to do with programming? And why were they everywhere at GitHub Universe? A lot of you asked, so I’m here to help explain.

node.Title: How we use GitHub to be more productive, collaborative, and secure
node.Description: Our engineering and security teams have done some incredible work in 2022. Let’s take a look at how we use GitHub to be more productive, build collaboratively, and shift security left.

node.Title: Creating an accessible search experience with the QueryBuilder component
node.Description: GitHub’s search inputs have several complex accessibility considerations. Let’s dive into what those are, how we addressed them, and talk about the standalone, reusable component that was ultimately built.

node.Title: Highlights from Git 2.39
node.Description: Another new release of Git is here to end the year! Take a look at some of our highlights on what's new in Git 2.39.

node.Title: Experiment: The hidden costs of waiting on slow build times
node.Description: How much does it really cost to buy more powerful cloud compute resources for development work? A lot less than you think.

node.Title: GitHub Availability Report: November 2022
node.Description: In November, we experienced two incidents that resulted in degraded performance across GitHub services. This report also sheds light into an incident that impacted Codespaces in October.

node.Title: To infinity and beyond: enabling the future of GitHub’s REST API with API versioning
node.Description: We’re introducing calendar-based versioning for our REST API, so we can keep evolving our API, whilst still giving integrators a smooth migration path and plenty of time to update their integrations.

node.Title: Exciting New GitHub Features Powering Machine Learning
node.Description: Discover the exciting enhancements in GitHub that empower Machine Learning practitioners to do more.

node.Title: How GitHub converts previously encrypted and unencrypted columns to ActiveRecord encrypted columns
node.Description: This post is the second part in a series about ActiveRecord::Encryption that shows how GitHub upgrades previously encrypted and unencrypted columns to ActiveRecord::Encryption.

node.Title: GitHub Availability Report: October 2022
node.Description: In October, we experienced four incidents that resulted in degraded performance across GitHub services. This report also sheds light into an incident that impacted Codespaces in September.

node.Title: Why and how GitHub encrypts sensitive database columns using ActiveRecord::Encryption
node.Description: You may know that GitHub encrypts your source code at rest, but you may not have known that we encrypt sensitive database columns as well. Read about our column encryption strategy and our decision to adopt the Rails column encryption standard.

node.Title: The Story of Scalar
node.Description: New to Git v2.38, Scalar is a built-in repository manager for large repos. Here, we’ll tell the story of how Scalar went from a rough VFS for Git successor to a fully-integrated Git tool, with all of the engineering lessons learned in the process.

node.Title: GitHub Availability Report: September 2022
node.Description: In September, we experienced one incident that resulted in degraded performance across GitHub services. We also experienced one incident resulting in significant impact to Codespaces. We are still investigating that incident and will include it in next month’s report. This report also sheds light into an incident that impacted Codespaces in August and an incident that impacted Actions in August.

node.Title: Highlights from Git 2.38
node.Description: Another new release of Git is here! Take a look at some of our highlights on what's new in Git 2.38.

node.Title: How we tripled max concurrent jobs to boost performance of GitHub Actions
node.Description: The GitHub Actions team has done lots of work to improve the performance and resource consumption of Actions on GHES in the past year.

node.Title: 8 things you didn’t know you could do with GitHub Copilot
node.Description: Developers all over the world are using GitHub Copilot to help speed up their development and increase developer productivity. With GitHub Copilot available to developers everywhere, we’ve found some fun and useful examples of how developers can use GitHub Copilot for things you may not be thinking about.

node.Title: Scaling Git’s garbage collection
node.Description: A tour of recent work to re-engineer Git’s garbage collection process to scale to our largest and most active repositories.

node.Title: GitHub Availability Report: August 2022
node.Description: In August, we experienced one incident resulting in significant impact to Codespaces. We’re still investigating that incident and will include it in next month’s report. This report also sheds light into an incident that impacted Codespaces in July.

node.Title: Contributing to open source at GitHub
node.Description: A software engineer’s personal journey to becoming an open source contributor.

node.Title: Git’s database internals V: scalability
node.Description: This fifth and final part of our blog series exploring Git's internals shows several strategies for scaling your Git repositories that match related database sharding techniques.

node.Title: Git’s database internals IV: distributed synchronization
node.Description: We're examining Git’s internals to help make your engineering system more efficient. This post views Git as a distributed database and looks into its synchronization techniques, specifically ‘git fetch’ and ‘git push’.

node.Title: Git’s Database Internals III: File History Queries
node.Description: Git’s file history queries use specialized algorithms that are tailored to common developer behavior. Level up your history spelunking skills by learning how different history modes behave and which ones to use when you need them.

node.Title: Git’s database internals II: commit history queries
node.Description: This post explores Git commit history as a database where ‘git log’ is the query language. Learn about Git’s custom query index – the commit-graph file – and how to make sure it's enabled in your repositories.

node.Title: Git’s database internals I: packed object store
node.Description: This blog series will examine Git’s internals to help make your engineering system more efficient. Part I discusses how Git stores its data in packfiles using custom compression techniques.

node.Title: Introducing Trilogy: a new database adapter for Ruby on Rails
node.Description: We've open sourced Trilogy, the database adapter we use to connect Ruby on Rails to MySQL-compatible database servers.

node.Title: GitHub Availability Report: July 2022
node.Description: In July, we experienced one incident that resulted in degraded performance for Codespaces. This report also acknowledges two incidents that impacted multiple GitHub.com services in June.

node.Title: Introducing even more security enhancements to npm
node.Description: New npm security enhancements include an improved login and publish experience with the npm CLI, connected GitHub and Twitter accounts, and a new CLI command to verify the integrity of packages in npm.

node.Title: 6 strategic ways to level up your CI/CD pipeline
node.Description: From incorporating accessibility testing to implementing blue-green deployment models, here are six practical and strategic ways to improve your CI/CD pipeline.

node.Title: GitHub Availability Report: June 2022
node.Description: In June, we experienced four incidents resulting in significant impact to multiple GitHub.com services. This report also sheds light into an incident that impacted several GitHub.com services in May.

node.Title: Write Better Commits, Build Better Projects
node.Description: High-quality Git commits are the key to a maintainable and collaborative open- or closed-source project. Learn strategies to improve and use commits to streamline your development process.

node.Title: Improve Git monorepo performance with a file system monitor
node.Description: Monorepo performance can suffer due to the sheer number of files in your working directory. Git’s new builtin file system monitor makes it easy to speed up monorepo performance.

node.Title: Highlights from Git 2.37
node.Description: The open source Git project just released Git 2.37. Take a look at some of our highlights from the latest release.

node.Title: Prebuilding codespaces is generally available
node.Description: Teams and GHEC customers can now create blazing fast codespaces, even for your largest and most complex projects.

node.Title: Accelerating GitHub theme creation with color tooling
node.Description: Learn why the GitHub Design Infrastructure team built a dedicated color tool and how they use it to create new color palettes for GitHub.

node.Title: How we think about browsers
node.Description: Discover how GitHub thinks about browser support, look at usage patterns, and learn about the tools we use to make sure our customers are getting the best experience.

node.Title: Sunsetting Atom
node.Description: We are archiving Atom and all projects under the Atom organization for an official sunset on December 15, 2022.
